title: Engine 181 Wye Split Attack
question: Engine 181 is supplying a wye with 200’ of 2½” hose. Branch A is 150’ of 1¾” flowing 150 GPM with a fog nozzle at 50 PSI. Branch B is 200’ of 1¾” flowing 150 GPM with a fog nozzle at 50 PSI. What pump pressure should the operator set?

hoses: Branch A: 150’ 1¾” yellow attack line to front door
Branch B: 200’ 1¾” yellow attack line to side door
nozzles: Branch A fog nozzle, 150 GPM @ 50 PSI
Branch B fog nozzle, 150 GPM @ 50 PSI

formulaBreakdown: PP = FL + NP ± elevation + appliance loss
Supply FL = 2 × (300 / 100)^2 × 2 = 36 PSI, rounded to 35 PSI
Branch A FL = 15.5 × (150 / 100)^2 × 1.5 = 52.3 PSI, rounded to 52 PSI
Branch A total = 35 + 52 + 50 = 137 PSI, rounded to 140 PSI
Branch B FL = 15.5 × (150 / 100)^2 × 2 = 69.75 PSI, rounded to 70 PSI
Wye appliance loss = 0 PSI because total flow is 300 GPM
artPrompt: Create a vertical mobile-friendly firefighting training illustration for a phone display. Dark professional nighttime background, semi-isometric residential fireground diagram, realistic but simplified. Show Engine 181 on the street supplying a yellow wye near the front walkway with a 200’ 2½” blue supply hose. From the wye, show two yellow 1¾” attack lines: Branch A is 150’ 1¾” to the front door, labeled ‘A: 150’ 1¾ Fog 150 @ 50’; Branch B is 200’ 1¾” to the side door, labeled ‘B: 200’ 1¾ Fog 150 @ 50’. No direction arrows on hoses. Use large high-contrast labels, clean hose paths, readable phone layout, minimal clutter, and leave safe space at the bottom for the app question and answer UI.
